title: Proclamation No. 34, s. 1936
text:
PROCLAMATION NO. 34

ESTABLISHING A WATERSHED FOREST RESERVE IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF BALER, PROVINCE OF TAYABAS

Upon the recommendation of the Director of Forestry, approved by the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce, and pursuant to the provisions of section eighteen hundred and twenty-six of Act Numbered Twenty-seven hundred and eleven, known as the Revised Administrative Code, I, Manuel L. Quezon, President of the Philippines, hereby establish a watershed forest reserve in the Municipality of Baler, Province of Tayabas, and place said forest reserve under the administration and control of the Bureau of Forestry, which shall have the authority to regulate the cutting, collection, and removal of timber and other forest products therein in accordance with the Forest Law and regulations, for watershed protection and timber production, subject to private rights, if any there be, and to any all rights and privileges of exploration, occupation, purchase, or otherwise granted or reserved to citizens by provisions of the Act of Congress of July first, nineteen hundred and two, known as the Philippine Bill.

I, therefore, withdraw from sale or settlement, except as permitted or provided for in the aforesaid Act of Congress of July first, nineteen hundred and two, and hereby declare and proclaim to be a watershed forest reserve that portion of the public domain, situated in the Municipality of Baler, Province of Tayabas, Island of Luzon, and described in the Bureau of Forestry map F. R.-57, to wit:

Beginning at corner marked I on Bureau of Forestry map No. F. R.-57, a concrete monument, identical to corner 2, lot 1, Baler cadastre 185, B. L. case 2, which is S. 87ْ E., about 4,400 meters from the Poblacion, municipality of Baler, Tayabas; thence N. 6ْ E., 260 meters to corner 2, a malapiña tree, 60 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 226/21, identical to corner 17, block E, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project N. 14; thence N. 79ْ E., 630 meters to corner 3, a baton tree, 60 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 226/16, identical to corner 18, block E, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence following the seacoast in a general southeasterly direction about 670 meters to corner 4, a post, 15 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 226/12, identical to corner 2, block F. alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence following Natolo Creek downstream in a general northerly and northeasterly directions about 130 meters to corner 7, an unknown tree, 15 cm. diameter, identical to corner 1, block F, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence following the seacoast in a general easterly and southeasterly directions about 1,100 meters to corner 8, a manapo tree, 40 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 226/1-A, identical to corner 2, block G, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence S. 30ْ W., 620 meters to corner 9, a mayapis tree, 60 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 226/1-A, identical to corner 3, block G, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence S. 73ْ E., 420  meters to corner 10, an unknown tree, 30 cm. diameter, identical to corner 4, block G, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence N. 26ْ E., 640 meters to corner 11, an unknown tree, 25 cm. diameter, identical to corner 1, block G, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project N. 14; thence following the seacoast in a general southeasterly and southwesterly directions about 2,780 meters to corner 12, a point, at the mouth of Dicasalaria River; thence following Dicasalaria River upstream in a general southwesterly direction about 130 meters to corner 13, an agoho tree, 40 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 226/1, identical to corner 1, block H, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence S. 65ْ W., 400 meters to corner 14, an unknown tree, 25 cm. diameter, marked F. Z. 266/12, identical to corner 2, block H, alienable and disposable, Tayabas project No. 14; thence N. 30ْ E., 210 meters to corner 15, an igang tree, 15 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1469/23, on top of a ridge, south of a trail; thence N. 25ْ W., 350 meters to corner 16, a guisok tree, 20 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1469/14, on top of a ridge, south of a trail; thence N. 29ْ W., 210 meters to corner 17, a guisok tree, 40 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1469/8, on top of a ridge, south of a trail; thence N. 51ْ W., 260 meters to corner 18, a guisok tree, 25 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1469/1, on top of a ridge, north of a trail; thence N. 87ْ W., 220 meters to corner 19, a bibit tree, 25 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1466/20, on top of a ridge, north of a trail; thence N. 82ْ W., 200 meters to corner 20, a kulasi (gubat) tree, 25 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1468/46, on top of a ridge, on north side of a trail; thence S. 24ْ W., 270 meters to corner 21, a macaasim tree, 40 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1468/39, on top of a ridge; thence N. 88ْ W., 270 meters to corner 22, a tangile tree, 70 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1468/32, on top of a ridge; thence S. 84ْ W., 300 meters to corner 23, a tangile tree, 60 cm. diameter,marked F. R. 1468/25, on top of a ridge; thence N. 39ْ W., 210 meters to corner 24, an unknown tree, 40 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1468/19, on top of a ridge thence S. 81ْ W., 230 meters to corner 26, a malapototan tree, 30 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1468/3, on top of a ridge; thence N. 32ْ E., 100 meters to corner 27, an igang tree, 25 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1467/74, on top of a ridge; thence N. 56ْ W., 170 meters to corner 28, a malapototan tree, 60 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1467/66, on west bank of a creek; thence N. 89ْ W., 260 meters to corner 29, a red lauan tree, 90 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1467/60, on top of a ridge; thence S. 8ْ W., 160 meters to corner 30, a tangile tree, 50 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1467/61, on slope of a ridge; thence S. 73ْ W., 190 meters to corner 31, a manaring tree, 30 cm. diameter, marked F.R. 1467/50, on east bank of a creek; thence N. 6ْ E., 550 meters to corner 32, a palosapis tree, 60 cm. diameter, marked F. R. 1467/7, at junction of a trail and communal forest boundary; thence N. 6ْ E., 210 meters to corner 33, a duguan tree, identical to corner 13, Baler communal forest parcel I; thence following Puntian Creek downstream in a general northwesterly direction about 800 meters to corner 34, an unknown tree, identical to corner 1, Baler communal forest parcel I; thence N. 50ْ E., 200 meters to corner 1, the point of beginning.

Corners 1 to 11 are marked with the Bureau of Forestry official marking hatchet No. 23; corners 13 and 14 with the Bureau of Forestry official marking hatchet No. 151; corners 15 to 32 with the Bureau of Forestry Official marking hatchet No. 437; and corners 33 and 34 with the Bureau of Forestry official marking hatchet No. 23.

Containing an area of 430 hectares, more or less.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this fourth day of February, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-six, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the first.

[SEAL]

President of the Philippines

By the President:

Secretary of the Interior
